We come before You with humble hearts, seeking Your protection over the Gulf states, especially Florida, as Hurricane Milton approaches. Lord, You are the Creator of all things, and even the winds and the waves obey You. We ask that You calm this storm and shield the people in its path.

FYI, if you have an iPhone 14 or later, I would highly recommend upgrading to the latest iOS 18.x before the worst of the storm gets here to be able to make use of satellite messaging that works without cellular service nor Wi-Fi in emergencies, just in case that happens and it’s necessary. See apple link at bottom for how and to try the demo test to make sure you know it works.
The feature only got released September 16, 2024 when iOS 18 came out and it includes an ability to send iMessage or SMS text message via satellite if Wi-Fi or cellular is down, and not just to emergency services. It does require a view of the sky to work, so debatably would be more useful after pouring rain stops.
I would also recommend turning on Find My Friends and adding a couple extended family members just in case so they can at least know where you are or where you were last your phone battery dies. These are just things I’m doing, feel free not to, just wanted to advise in case others don’t know about it as I haven’t heard it mention much in any weather reporters prep list.
The satellite messaging is a three week old feature, so not many people know about it. It greatly helped a friend of mine in North Carolina near Asheville stay in touch with love ones when the cell towers and service failed.
